content.full_text:
bect10n Hb~U2 
Arranging for Contributions 
An association raises funds via 
fundraiser. 
Some 
of the funds are used to support the association's operating 
expenses and others are used to make contributions to a PAC 
which supports state ca 
idates. 
Ken and I concluded on March 
29, 1977, that a lobbyist for the association could not partici-
pate in the fundraiser since some of the funds contributed 
would be earmarked for the PAC which supports state candidates. 
Since Section 86202 prohibits 
lobbyist from making any contri-
butions to a state candidate, part 
ipation by the lobbyist in 
such an event is strictly prohibited.

sections.conclusion: A lobbyist for the association could not participate in the fundraiser since some of the funds contributed would be earmarked for the PAC which supports state candidates. Since Section 86202 prohibits a lobbyist from making any contributions to a state candidate, participation by the lobbyist in such an event is strictly prohibited.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a lobbyist participate in a fundraiser held by an association if some of the funds raised will be used to make contributions to a PAC supporting state candidates?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A lobbyist is prohibited from participating in a fundraiser where any portion of the funds will support a PAC that makes contributions to state candidates, as this violates Section 86202's ban on lobbyist contributions to state candidates.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional Q/A section headers but contains a clear question and conclusion within narrative text. Reference to 'Ken and I concluded' suggests internal FPPC discussion. Section heading 'Arranging for Contributions' visible. Some OCR quality issues in source text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a lobbyist participate in a fundraiser held by an association if some of the funds raised will be used to make contributions to a PAC supporting state candidates?

CONCLUSION: A lobbyist for the association could not participate in the fundraiser since some of the funds contributed would be earmarked for the PAC which supports state candidates. Since Section 86202 prohibits a lobbyist from making any contributions to a state candidate, participation by the lobbyist in such an event is strictly prohibited.
embedding.first_500_words: bect10n Hb~U2 Arranging for Contributions An association raises funds via fundraiser. Some of the funds are used to support the association's operating expenses and others are used to make contributions to a PAC which supports state ca idates. Ken and I concluded on March 29, 1977, that a lobbyist for the association could not partici- pate in the fundraiser since some of the funds contributed would be earmarked for the PAC which supports state candidates. Since Section 86202 prohibits lobbyist from making any contri- butions to a state candidate, part ipation by the lobbyist in such an event is strictly prohibited.
embedding.summary: The FPPC advised that a lobbyist cannot participate in an association's fundraiser if any of the proceeds will be directed toward a PAC supporting state candidates, as this would violate the prohibition on lobbyist contributions to state candidates under Section 86202.
